improvement_directive: Prove a feasible adaptive HT allocation rule whose cumulative Neyman regret is bounded by the same closed-band frontier K up to the sharp universal constant, using the same interior moment and tangent-strength class. Reconcile the strict-interior lower-bound constant with the closed-band frontier by either an interior approximation argument or an upper bound stated on the identical closed class.
flagship_directive: Prove a constant-sharp adaptive upper bound for the same HT Neyman-regret functional matching the K frontier on the identical regular band.
critique: The note does prove a genuine superpopulation Horvitz-Thompson Omega(log T) minimax converse on a tangent-regular bounded-outcome class, including actual bounded local alternatives and a local information quotient. The headline should be read as rate-sharp, not sharp minimax: the lower bound carries only a universal unspecified constant and the global theorem uses a strict-interior supremum rather than the closed-band frontier K. The closed-form frontier calculation identifies the local lower-bound complexity, but it is not a constant-sharp minimax value because no matching upper bound is proved. The assumptions are restrictive but standard regularity/localization conditions rather than question-begging, so they do not reduce the result below field level.
